title: Find products that have no associated product info
sql:
SELECT p.Pid, p.ASIN
FROM PRODUCT p
LEFT JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON p.Pid = pi.Pid
WHERE pi.Pid IS NULL;
explanation: Identifies products that exist in the PRODUCT table but lack detailed information in PRODUCT_INFO.

title: Search for products with 'Harry Potter' in the title
sql:
SELECT p.Pid, ASIN, title
FROM PRODUCT p
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON p.Pid = pi.Pid
WHERE pi.title LIKE '%Harry Potter%';
explanation: Performs a case-sensitive substring search for book titles containing "Harry Potter".

title: List all distinct product groups (categories) available
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT p_group
FROM PRODUCT_INFO
WHERE p_group IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY p_group;
explanation: Shows the unique types of products in the database (e.g., 'Book', 'DVD', 'Music').

title: Count total number of products per group
sql:
SELECT p_group, COUNT(*) AS product_count
FROM PRODUCT_INFO
GROUP BY p_group
ORDER BY product_count DESC;
explanation: Provides a breakdown of how many items exist in each product category.

title: Get the top 100 best-selling products (lowest salesrank)
sql:
SELECT p.Pid, p.ASIN, pi.title, pi.salesrank
FROM PRODUCT p
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON p.Pid = pi.Pid
WHERE pi.salesrank IS NOT NULL AND pi.salesrank > 0
ORDER BY pi.salesrank ASC
LIMIT 100;
explanation: Retrieves the 100 products with the highest sales (lowest sales rank number, e.g., #1 bestseller).

title: Find products with no sales rank recorded
explanation: Lists products that either have never sold or lack sales rank data.

title: Average sales rank by product group
sql:
SELECT p_group, AVG(salesrank) AS avg_salesrank
FROM PRODUCT_INFO
WHERE salesrank > 0
GROUP BY p_group;
explanation: Calculates the typical sales rank performance for each category (lower is better).

title: Products with sales rank improvement potential (high rank number)
sql:
SELECT p.Pid, p.ASIN, pi.title, pi.salesrank
FROM PRODUCT p
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON p.Pid = pi.Pid
WHERE pi.salesrank > 100000
ORDER BY pi.salesrank DESC
LIMIT 50;
explanation: Finds the bottom-selling products (very high rank numbers) that might need marketing attention.

title: Median sales rank calculation using window function
sql:
WITH ranked AS (
  SELECT salesrank, 
         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY salesrank) AS rn,
         COUNT(*) OVER () AS total
  FROM PRODUCT_INFO
  WHERE salesrank > 0
)
SELECT AVG(salesrank) AS median_salesrank
FROM ranked
WHERE rn IN (FLOOR((total+1)/2), CEIL((total+1)/2));
explanation: Computes the median sales rank across all products, which is more robust to outliers than average.

title: Find all categories and their parent categories
sql:
SELECT c.cat_id, c.cat_nome, p.cat_nome AS parent_category
FROM CATEGORY c
LEFT JOIN CATEGORY p ON c.pcat_id = p.cat_id
ORDER BY c.cat_id;
explanation: Displays the category tree showing each category and its immediate parent.

title: List all products belonging to a specific category (e.g., 'Science Fiction')
sql:
SELECT p.Pid, p.ASIN, pi.title
FROM PRODUCT p
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON p.Pid = pi.Pid
JOIN PRODUCT_CAT pc ON p.Pid = pc.Pid
JOIN CATEGORY c ON pc.id_final_cat = c.cat_id
WHERE c.cat_nome = 'Science Fiction';
explanation: Retrieves all products classified under the "Science Fiction" category.

title: Count products per category (leaf nodes)
sql:
SELECT c.cat_id, c.cat_nome, COUNT(pc.Pid) AS product_count
FROM CATEGORY c
LEFT JOIN PRODUCT_CAT pc ON c.cat_id = pc.id_final_cat
GROUP BY c.cat_id, c.cat_nome
ORDER BY product_count DESC;
explanation: Shows how many products are directly assigned to each final category.

title: Find categories with no products assigned
sql:
SELECT c.cat_id, c.cat_nome
FROM CATEGORY c
LEFT JOIN PRODUCT_CAT pc ON c.cat_id = pc.id_final_cat
WHERE pc.id_final_cat IS NULL;
explanation: Identifies unused categories that could be removed or need product assignment.

title: Products that belong to multiple categories
sql:
SELECT p.Pid, p.ASIN, pi.title, COUNT(pc.id_final_cat) AS category_count
FROM PRODUCT p
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON p.Pid = pi.Pid
JOIN PRODUCT_CAT pc ON p.Pid = pc.Pid
GROUP BY p.Pid, p.ASIN, pi.title
HAVING COUNT(pc.id_final_cat) > 1
ORDER BY category_count DESC;
explanation: Finds products that are cross-listed in more than one category.

title: Top 5 categories with highest average product rating
sql:
SELECT c.cat_id, c.cat_nome, AVG(pi.avg_rating) AS avg_category_rating
FROM CATEGORY c
JOIN PRODUCT_CAT pc ON c.cat_id = pc.id_final_cat
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON pc.Pid = pi.Pid
WHERE pi.avg_rating IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY c.cat_id, c.cat_nome
ORDER BY avg_category_rating DESC
LIMIT 5;
explanation: Ranks categories by the average rating of their products, indicating overall customer satisfaction per category.

title: Find all similar products for a given product ASIN
sql:
SELECT p2.ASIN AS similar_ASIN, pi2.title AS similar_title
FROM PRODUCT p1
JOIN SIMILAR_PRODUCT sp ON p1.Pid = sp.Pid
JOIN PRODUCT p2 ON sp.ASIN_SIM = p2.ASIN
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi2 ON p2.Pid = pi2.Pid
WHERE p1.ASIN = 'B000FA5E4K';
explanation: Retrieves the list of similar/recommended products for a specific ASIN (e.g., "B000FA5E4K").

title: Count how many times each product is listed as similar to others
sql:
SELECT p.ASIN, pi.title, COUNT(sp.Pid) AS times_recommended
FROM PRODUCT p
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON p.Pid = pi.Pid
LEFT JOIN SIMILAR_PRODUCT sp ON p.ASIN = sp.ASIN_SIM
GROUP BY p.Pid, p.ASIN, pi.title
ORDER BY times_recommended DESC
LIMIT 100;
explanation: Finds the most frequently co-recommended products, indicating popularity or strong relationships.

title: Find mutual similar relationships (A similar to B and B similar to A)
sql:
SELECT p1.ASIN AS product_A, p2.ASIN AS product_B
FROM SIMILAR_PRODUCT sp1
JOIN SIMILAR_PRODUCT sp2 ON sp1.ASIN_SIM = sp2.ASIN_SIM AND sp1.Pid = sp2.Pid
JOIN PRODUCT p1 ON sp1.Pid = p1.Pid
JOIN PRODUCT p2 ON sp1.ASIN_SIM = p2.ASIN
WHERE EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 FROM SIMILAR_PRODUCT sp3
  WHERE sp3.Pid = p2.Pid AND sp3.ASIN_SIM = p1.ASIN
);
explanation: Identifies pairs of products that both recommend each other as similar.

title: Get all reviews for a specific product with helpfulness ratio
sql:
SELECT rev_id, date, cutomer, rating, votes, helpful,
       CASE WHEN votes > 0 THEN ROUND(helpful * 100.0 / votes, 1) ELSE 0 END AS helpful_pct
FROM REVIEW
WHERE Pid = 12345
ORDER BY date DESC;
explanation: Displays all customer reviews for a product, including the percentage of people who found each review helpful.

title: Top 10 products with the highest average rating (minimum 50 reviews)
sql:
SELECT p.Pid, p.ASIN, pi.title, pi.avg_rating, pi.reviews
FROM PRODUCT p
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON p.Pid = pi.Pid
WHERE pi.reviews >= 50
ORDER BY pi.avg_rating DESC
LIMIT 10;
explanation: Finds the highest-rated products that have a statistically significant number of reviews.

title: Products with most 5-star reviews
sql:
SELECT p.Pid, p.ASIN, pi.title, COUNT(r.rev_id) AS five_star_count
FROM PRODUCT p
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON p.Pid = pi.Pid
JOIN REVIEW r ON p.Pid = r.Pid
WHERE r.rating = 5
GROUP BY p.Pid, p.ASIN, pi.title
ORDER BY five_star_count DESC
LIMIT 20;
explanation: Ranks products based on the sheer volume of perfect ratings.

title: Identify products with high rating but low helpful reviews
sql:
SELECT p.Pid, p.ASIN, pi.title, pi.avg_rating,
       AVG(r.helpful * 1.0 / NULLIF(r.votes,0)) AS avg_helpfulness
FROM PRODUCT p
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON p.Pid = pi.Pid
JOIN REVIEW r ON p.Pid = r.Pid
GROUP BY p.Pid, p.ASIN, pi.title, pi.avg_rating
HAVING pi.avg_rating > 4.0 AND AVG(r.helpful * 1.0 / NULLIF(r.votes,0)) < 0.5;
explanation: Detects products that have high star ratings but where the reviews are not considered helpful by other users (potential fake review indicator).

title: Monthly review volume trend for a product
sql:
SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', date) AS month, COUNT(*) AS review_count, AVG(rating) AS avg_rating
FROM REVIEW
WHERE Pid = 12345
GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', date)
ORDER BY month;
explanation: Shows how many reviews a product receives per month and how the average rating changes over time.

title: Find customers who have reviewed the same product multiple times
sql:
SELECT Pid, cutomer, COUNT(*) AS review_count
FROM REVIEW
GROUP BY Pid, cutomer
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
explanation: Identifies duplicate reviews by the same customer on a single product (possible data issue or updated reviews).

title: Reviews with the highest 'helpful' vote counts
sql:
SELECT r.rev_id, p.ASIN, pi.title, r.cutomer, r.helpful, r.votes, r.rating
FROM REVIEW r
JOIN PRODUCT p ON r.Pid = p.Pid
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON p.Pid = pi.Pid
WHERE r.helpful > 100
ORDER BY r.helpful DESC
LIMIT 50;
explanation: Retrieves the most influential reviews based on the number of people who found them helpful.

title: Distribution of ratings (1-5 stars) across all products
sql:
SELECT rating, COUNT(*) AS count,
       ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (), 2) AS percentage
FROM REVIEW
GROUP BY rating
ORDER BY rating DESC;
explanation: Provides an overall histogram of star ratings to see if reviews skew positive or negative.

title: Find products where average rating differs significantly from recent reviews
sql:
WITH recent_avg AS (
  SELECT Pid, AVG(rating) AS recent_avg_rating
  FROM REVIEW
  WHERE date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days'
  GROUP BY Pid
)
SELECT p.Pid, p.ASIN, pi.title, pi.avg_rating AS overall_avg, ra.recent_avg_rating
FROM PRODUCT p
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON p.Pid = pi.Pid
JOIN recent_avg ra ON p.Pid = ra.Pid
WHERE ABS(pi.avg_rating - ra.recent_avg_rating) > 1.0
ORDER BY ABS(pi.avg_rating - ra.recent_avg_rating) DESC;
explanation: Identifies products whose recent rating trend is moving up or down compared to their historical average.

title: Find the most frequently purchased together items (via similar products)
sql:
SELECT p1.ASIN AS product, p2.ASIN AS also_bought, COUNT(*) AS frequency
FROM SIMILAR_PRODUCT sp1
JOIN SIMILAR_PRODUCT sp2 ON sp1.Pid = sp2.Pid AND sp1.ASIN_SIM < sp2.ASIN_SIM
JOIN PRODUCT p1 ON sp1.ASIN_SIM = p1.ASIN
JOIN PRODUCT p2 ON sp2.ASIN_SIM = p2.ASIN
GROUP BY p1.ASIN, p2.ASIN
ORDER BY frequency DESC
LIMIT 50;
explanation: Uses similarity relationships to infer frequently co-purchased item pairs (market basket analysis).

title: Rank products within each category by sales rank
sql:
SELECT c.cat_nome, p.ASIN, pi.title, pi.salesrank,
       RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY c.cat_id ORDER BY pi.salesrank ASC) AS rank_in_category
FROM PRODUCT p
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON p.Pid = pi.Pid
JOIN PRODUCT_CAT pc ON p.Pid = pc.Pid
JOIN CATEGORY c ON pc.id_final_cat = c.cat_id
WHERE pi.salesrank IS NOT NULL AND pi.salesrank > 0;
explanation: Shows the bestseller position of each product relative only to other items in its specific category.

title: Identify "cold start" products (new with few reviews) but good sales rank
sql:
SELECT p.Pid, p.ASIN, pi.title, pi.salesrank, pi.reviews
FROM PRODUCT p
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON p.Pid = pi.Pid
WHERE pi.salesrank BETWEEN 1 AND 1000
  AND (pi.reviews IS NULL OR pi.reviews < 10)
ORDER BY pi.salesrank ASC;
explanation: Finds best-selling products that lack sufficient reviews, representing opportunities for early review solicitation.

title: Correlation between number of similar products and sales rank
sql:
SELECT sim_count_range, AVG(pi.salesrank) AS avg_salesrank, COUNT(*) AS product_count
FROM (
  SELECT p.Pid, COUNT(sp.ASIN_SIM) AS sim_count,
         CASE
           WHEN COUNT(sp.ASIN_SIM) = 0 THEN '0 similar'
           WHEN COUNT(sp.ASIN_SIM) BETWEEN 1 AND 5 THEN '1-5 similar'
           WHEN COUNT(sp.ASIN_SIM) BETWEEN 6 AND 20 THEN '6-20 similar'
           ELSE '>20 similar'
         END AS sim_count_range
  FROM PRODUCT p
  LEFT JOIN SIMILAR_PRODUCT sp ON p.Pid = sp.Pid
  GROUP BY p.Pid
) sim_stats
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON sim_stats.Pid = pi.Pid
WHERE pi.salesrank > 0
GROUP BY sim_count_range
ORDER BY avg_salesrank;
explanation: Investigates whether having more similar/recommended products correlates with better sales rank.

title: Most reviewed products per month in the last year
sql:
WITH monthly_reviews AS (
  SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', date) AS month, Pid, COUNT(*) AS rev_count,
         RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY DATE_TRUNC('month', date) ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) AS rnk
  FROM REVIEW
  WHERE date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
  GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', date), Pid
)
SELECT mr.month, p.ASIN, pi.title, mr.rev_count
FROM monthly_reviews mr
JOIN PRODUCT p ON mr.Pid = p.Pid
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON p.Pid = pi.Pid
WHERE mr.rnk <= 5
ORDER BY mr.month DESC, mr.rnk ASC;
explanation: Shows the top 5 most buzzworthy products each month based on review activity.

title: Products that are top 10% in sales rank within their group
sql:
WITH group_percentiles AS (
  SELECT Pid, p_group, salesrank,
         PERCENT_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY p_group ORDER BY salesrank ASC) AS pct_rank
  FROM PRODUCT_INFO
  WHERE salesrank > 0
)
SELECT pi.Pid, p.ASIN, pi.title, pi.p_group, pi.salesrank
FROM group_percentiles gp
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON gp.Pid = pi.Pid
JOIN PRODUCT p ON pi.Pid = p.Pid
WHERE gp.pct_rank <= 0.10
ORDER BY pi.p_group, pi.salesrank;
explanation: Identifies the best-selling items within each product category (top 10% of sales performance).

title: Average rating by day of week for reviews posted
sql:
SELECT EXTRACT(DOW FROM date) AS day_of_week,
       TO_CHAR(date, 'Day') AS day_name,
       AVG(rating) AS avg_rating,
       COUNT(*) AS review_count
FROM REVIEW
GROUP BY EXTRACT(DOW FROM date), TO_CHAR(date, 'Day')
ORDER BY day_of_week;
explanation: Checks if ratings tend to be higher or lower depending on the day of the week a review was written.

title: Find products that are similar to the top 10 bestsellers
sql:
WITH top_products AS (
  SELECT Pid FROM PRODUCT_INFO WHERE salesrank > 0 ORDER BY salesrank ASC LIMIT 10
)
SELECT DISTINCT p.ASIN, pi.title
FROM top_products tp
JOIN SIMILAR_PRODUCT sp ON tp.Pid = sp.Pid
JOIN PRODUCT p ON sp.ASIN_SIM = p.ASIN
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON p.Pid = pi.Pid
WHERE p.Pid NOT IN (SELECT Pid FROM top_products)
LIMIT 100;
explanation: Recommends items that are frequently similar to current bestsellers (a simple recommendation engine).

title: Create a view for "Books" category with all relevant data
sql:
CREATE VIEW v_books AS
SELECT p.Pid, p.ASIN, pi.title, pi.salesrank, pi.avg_rating, pi.reviews,
       c.cat_nome AS category_name
FROM PRODUCT p
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON p.Pid = pi.Pid
JOIN PRODUCT_CAT pc ON p.Pid = pc.Pid
JOIN CATEGORY c ON pc.id_final_cat = c.cat_id
WHERE c.cat_nome = 'Books' OR c.cat_nome LIKE '%Book%';
explanation: Creates a convenient view for querying only book products, simplifying future analysis.

title: For each customer, find their average rating given
sql:
SELECT cutomer, COUNT(*) AS reviews_written, AVG(rating) AS avg_rating_given,
       SUM(helpful) AS total_helpful_votes
FROM REVIEW
GROUP BY cutomer
ORDER BY reviews_written DESC
LIMIT 100;
explanation: Profiles the most active reviewers by their rating tendencies and helpfulness impact.

title: Products with no reviews in the last 6 months
sql:
SELECT p.Pid, p.ASIN, pi.title, MAX(r.date) AS last_review_date
FROM PRODUCT p
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON p.Pid = pi.Pid
LEFT JOIN REVIEW r ON p.Pid = r.Pid
GROUP BY p.Pid, p.ASIN, pi.title
HAVING MAX(r.date) < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '6 months' OR MAX(r.date) IS NULL;
explanation: Identifies products that are losing review momentum and may need a promotional boost.

title: Average helpful votes per review by product group
sql:
SELECT pi.p_group, AVG(r.helpful) AS avg_helpful_votes, AVG(r.votes) AS avg_total_votes
FROM REVIEW r
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON r.Pid = pi.Pid
GROUP BY pi.p_group
ORDER BY avg_helpful_votes DESC;
explanation: Compares the engagement level of reviews across different product categories.

title: Find all leaf categories under a specific parent category
sql:
WITH RECURSIVE subcats AS (
  SELECT cat_id FROM CATEGORY WHERE cat_nome = 'Electronics'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT c.cat_id FROM CATEGORY c JOIN subcats s ON c.pcat_id = s.cat_id
)
SELECT c.cat_id, c.cat_nome
FROM subcats s
JOIN CATEGORY c ON s.cat_id = c.cat_id
WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM CATEGORY child WHERE child.pcat_id = c.cat_id);
explanation: Retrieves only the bottom-most categories (leaf nodes) under a high-level category like "Electronics".

title: Products that have a sales rank but no categories assigned
sql:
SELECT p.Pid, p.ASIN, pi.title, pi.salesrank
FROM PRODUCT p
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON p.Pid = pi.Pid
LEFT JOIN PRODUCT_CAT pc ON p.Pid = pc.Pid
WHERE pc.id_final_cat IS NULL AND pi.salesrank > 0;
explanation: Finds active selling products that are missing category classification (data quality issue).

title: Reviews that mention "battery" in a hypothetical review text column (if existed)
sql:
-- This is a conceptual query; assumes a 'review_text' column exists.
SELECT r.rev_id, p.ASIN, pi.title, r.rating, r.cutomer
FROM REVIEW r
JOIN PRODUCT p ON r.Pid = p.Pid
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON p.Pid = pi.Pid
-- WHERE r.review_text LIKE '%battery%'
ORDER BY r.date DESC;
explanation: Searches for reviews containing a specific keyword to understand product sentiment about a feature (e.g., battery life). (Commented out since column not present.)

title: Total downloads (assuming 'downloads' counts digital purchases) per product group
sql:
SELECT pi.p_group, SUM(pi.downloads) AS total_downloads
FROM PRODUCT_INFO pi
GROUP BY pi.p_group
ORDER BY total_downloads DESC;
explanation: Aggregates download metrics to see which product categories are most popular in digital format.

title: Find products where the number of similar items is greater than the number of reviews
sql:
SELECT p.Pid, p.ASIN, pi.title, pi.reviews, COUNT(sp.ASIN_SIM) AS similar_count
FROM PRODUCT p
JOIN PRODUCT_INFO pi ON p.Pid = pi.Pid
LEFT JOIN SIMILAR_PRODUCT sp ON p.Pid = sp.Pid
GROUP BY p.Pid, p.ASIN, pi.title, pi.reviews
HAVING COUNT(sp.ASIN_SIM) > pi.reviews
ORDER BY similar_count DESC;
explanation: Identifies products that have a rich set of recommendations but very few actual customer reviews.

title: Top 10 most helpful reviewers overall
sql:
SELECT cutomer, SUM(helpful) AS total_helpful_votes, COUNT(*) AS reviews_count
FROM REVIEW
GROUP BY cutomer
ORDER BY total_helpful_votes DESC
LIMIT 10;
explanation: Ranks reviewers based on the total helpful votes they have accumulated across all their reviews.
